title: Australian insurance firms back sports and community events as sponsorship strategy accelerates
full_text:
Australian insurance firms have been deepening their sports and community sponsorship commitments at a notable pace. Howden strengthened its partnership with the Victoria Racing Club to include naming rights to what is now known as Howden Victoria Derby Day. Chubb renewed its position as Official Insurance Partner of the Australian Open, with the deal providing brand exposure through the tournament's Close Call electronic review system. NRMA has served as Cricket Australia's insurance partner since 2022, covering both men's and women's national teams and community cricket across the country. Health Partners became Port Adelaide FC's Official Health Insurance Partner, featuring across the club's game day experience and digital platforms.
MGA Insurance Group is the latest to extend its footprint, announcing it has signed on as major sponsor of the inaugural Flinders to Fleurieu Rally, scheduled for September 20-25, 2026, and formally titled the Operation Flinders to Fleurieu Rally. The sponsorship sits within a broader motorsport strategy: Motorsport Australia appointed MGA as its customer-facing insurance broker partner in 2025, alongside London-based Miller Insurance, to build what the governing body described as a world-class insurance and risk management model for the sport. MGA expanded that partnership in February 2026 to include support for development programs including FIA Girls on Track, First Gear and Women's Introduction to Motorsport. The rally sponsorship extends that motorsport footprint into event sponsorship.
What distinguishes the MGA sponsorship from a standard event placement is its alignment with the firm's geographic footprint and an existing charitable relationship. MGA Insurance Group, founded in Adelaide in 1975, operates 38 offices across Australia and counts regional South Australia as its home market. The rally route runs through Quorn, Clare and Victor Harbor - MGA operates a branch in Clare, one of the towns on the route. The event passes through 16 council regions between the Flinders Ranges and the Fleurieu Peninsula.
The charity at the centre of the event, Operation Flinders Foundation, is not a new relationship for MGA. The MGA Whittles Community Foundation - established in 2014 as a formalisation of a profit-allocation model dating to 2008 - has previously donated $50,000 to Operation Flinders, making the rally sponsorship an escalation of an existing commitment rather than a new one. The SA government granted Operation Flinders $150,000 earlier this year to expand its Adventure Mentoring program, which has engaged more than 70 young people in care through a partnership with the Department for Child Protection.
Operation Flinders has run remote outback adventure programs for at-risk young people aged 13-18 for more than 30 years, with participants trekking up to 100km over eight days in the northern Flinders Ranges. More than 10,000 young people have gone through the programs to date.
Operation Flinders chair and South Australian police commissioner Grant Stevens said the geography of the rally made the sponsorship tangible rather than symbolic. "Many of the young people who participate in our programs come from regional centres such as Quorn, Clare and Victor Harbor, making this event particularly meaningful. Operation Flinders changes lives. Every year, we see young people overcome significant challenges and build resilience, connection and purpose," he said.
The inaugural rally is billed as potentially Australia's largest gravel special stage rally, capped at 100 vehicles covering 2WD and 4WD Classic Rally Cars, with approximately 900km of competitive distance across daylight, closed-road special stages and a further 800km of transport. The event begins with a community celebration in Quorn before competitors move south, with a public car display in Clare on Wednesday evening. It also forms part of a Trans-Tasman series honouring Ed Mulligan, drawing Australian and New Zealand teams. No independent economic impact estimates were available at the time of publication.
